subtitle: At the rain-swollen Hydaspes, a Punjabi king met an empire without bowing his head.

panels.shot: low-angle wide of a war-elephant and rider against storm clouds over a vast river plain
panels.action: King Porus, tall and resolute, stands armored upon his war-elephant at the edge of a swollen river plain, monsoon clouds heavy above, distant silhouettes of spears and cavalry hinting at the waiting clash.

dialogue.text: Hydaspes, 326 BCE: here the king called Porus faced Alexander across a raging river, and met storm with calm eyes.

i18n.hi: ३२६ ईसा पूर्व, झेलम के तट: यहीं राजा पोरस ने उफनती नदी पार सिकंदर का सामना किया, तूफ़ान के सामने शांत नज़रें।

panels.shot: epic bird's-eye over the flooded Hydaspes, its banks and far plains
panels.action: The Hydaspes River surges wide and fierce through the Punjab plain, its brown waters swollen by rain, with forests and scattered villages along the far banks under a brooding sky.

dialogue.text: The Hydaspes was in full flood, a living wall of water between Macedon and Punjab, between Alexander’s march and Porus’s realm.

i18n.hi: झेलम उफान पर थी, पानी की जिंदा दीवार—एक तरफ मकदूनिया, दूसरी तरफ पंजाब; एक ओर सिकंदर की चढ़ाई, दूसरी ओर पोरस की धरती।

panels.shot: medium close-up of Porus in council, side-lit, map spread before him
panels.action: Inside a simple but stately Punjabi war pavilion, King Porus studies a rough map scratched on a board, his face calm yet firm, while unseen chieftains sit around him in shadow.

dialogue.text: Greek writers later named him Porus; in the land he ruled he was king among many chiefs, guarding his stretch of the Hydaspes with elephants and disciplined footmen.

i18n.hi: यूनानियों ने उसे ‘पोरस’ लिखा; अपने देश में वह कई सरदारों के बीच महाराजा था, झेलम के अपने हिस्से की रखवाली हाथियों और सधे पैदलों से करता।

panels.shot: sweeping wide of Porus’s army arrayed along the riverbank, Porus elevated on his elephant in the center
panels.action: On the near bank of the river, Porus sits high on his armored elephant, overlooking lines of infantry, chariots, and more elephants holding position, all facing the roaring waters and the unseen enemy beyond.

dialogue.text: He knew the tales of Alexander’s long advance from the west. Yet at Hydaspes he chose not the open field, but the river, to blunt the charge of foreign horsemen.

i18n.hi: उसे सिकंदर की पश्चिम से बढ़ती चढ़ाई की कहानियाँ पता थीं। पर झेलम पर उसने खुला मैदान नहीं, नदी चुनी—विदेशी घुड़सवारों की रफ्तार तोड़ने के लिए।

panels.shot: distant wide shot from Porus’s bank showing, across the river, ghostly shapes of Alexander’s distant camp and cavalry patrols in the rain
panels.action: Across the broad river, faint through curtains of rain, tents and watchfires of the Macedonian camp glow, while mounted scouts ride the far bank, their forms blurred by mist.

dialogue.text: Alexander watched from the farther bank. For days both sides stared across rain and flood, two commanders searching the river for weakness in each other’s will.

i18n.hi: सिकंदर दूसरी किनारी से देखता रहा। कई दिन दोनों सेनाएँ बारिश के पार एक-दूसरे को पहचानती रहीं, दो सेनानायक नदी में नहीं, एक-दूसरे की जिद में कचोट ढूँढते।

panels.shot: medium shot of Porus at dawn, silhouetted, watching the far bank with narrowed eyes
panels.action: At a gray dawn, Porus stands beside his elephant, cloak damp, eyes narrowed toward the far shore as thunderclouds roll, his hand resting on the beast’s flank in quiet resolve.

dialogue.text: The Greek sources praise Alexander’s guile, yet they admit Porus watched him closely, matching feint with patience, refusing to be drawn from the bank too soon.

i18n.hi: यूनानी लेखक सिकंदर की चालाकी की तारीफ करते हैं, पर मानते हैं कि पोरस हर चाल पर नजर रखे, हर झूठी हरकत के बदले धीरज से, किनारे से जल्दी हटने से इनकार।

panels.shot: wide shot of a stormy night, lightning revealing Porus’s distant lookouts and the hint of movement upstream
panels.action: Under a stormy night sky split by lightning, Porus’s pickets stand along the bank with torches; far upstream, barely visible, a smear of shadow shows Macedonian troops moving with rafts and horses.

dialogue.text: One night, in rain and darkness, Alexander stole men upstream, crossing where an island broke the river. Greek chronicles dwell on his daring; they also show Porus reacting with speed.

i18n.hi: एक रात, बारिश और अँधेरे में, सिकंदर अपने चुने लोगों को ऊपर की ओर ले गया, जहाँ एक टापू ने नदी चीर दी थी, वहीं से पार। यूनानी लिखत उसके साहस पर रुकते हैं, पर पोरस की तेज जवाबी चाल भी दिखाते हैं।

panels.shot: dynamic low-angle as Porus mounts his elephant, soldiers hurrying around him
panels.action: Alerted by messengers, Porus grasps the rope and mounts his war-elephant in a smooth practiced motion, armored guards rushing to their positions as the camp stirs from sleep into ordered movement.

dialogue.text: When word came that the enemy had crossed, Porus did not scatter in panic. He gathered his core, chose his ground, and marched to meet them inland from the river.

i18n.hi: जैसे ही खबर आई कि दुश्मन ने नदी पार कर ली, पोरस बिखरा नहीं। उसने अपने मुख्य दल को समेटा, जगह चुनी और नदी से हटकर भीतर की तरफ उन्हें रोकने चला।

panels.shot: sweeping wide of the battlefield plain, rain-soaked, with Porus’s war line facing an unseen foe beyond the frame
panels.action: On a waterlogged plain dotted with trees and pools, Porus’s infantry stand in ranks, his elephants spaced between them like living towers, as rain falls and the horizon shimmers with approaching cavalry and phalanx shapes.

dialogue.text: The field of Hydaspes was no open desert. Mud and monsoon weighed down man and beast; elephants loomed between lines, a different kind of terror than any Macedonian had yet faced.

i18n.hi: झेलम का मैदान सूखा रेगिस्तान नहीं था। कीचड़ और मानसून ने आदमी–घोड़े दोनों को भारी कर दिया; हाथी दोनों पंक्तियों के बीच खड़े—ऐसा डर, जो किसी मकदूनी ने पहले नहीं देखा था।

panels.shot: medium close-up on Porus atop his elephant, calm amid tension, his hand raised in command
panels.action: Porus sits straight-backed on his elephant in the center of his line, hand raised in a measured gesture as he gives a signal, his face composed, the strain in the men below him clear.

dialogue.speaker: king_porus_puru
dialogue.text: Hold your line. Let their charge break on our silence.

i18n.hi: पाँति मत तोड़ो। उनकी टक्कर हमारी चुप पर टूटने दो।

panels.shot: chaotic wide showing Porus’s elephants advancing, Macedonian cavalry wheeling to the flanks in the distance
panels.action: Porus’s front line of elephants lurches forward through muddy ground, trunks raised, while in the hazy background Macedonian cavalry can be seen sweeping around the flanks of the Punjabi formation.

dialogue.text: Arrian writes that Alexander refused to meet the elephants head on. He sent horsemen to the flanks, while his phalanx held firm against Porus’s advancing line.

i18n.hi: एर्रियन लिखता है कि सिकंदर ने हाथियों से सीधी भिड़ंत नहीं ली। उसने घुड़सवारों को दोनों किनारों पर घुमाया, और फालैंक्स को पोरस की बढ़ती रेखा के सामने जमाए रखा।

panels.shot: medium shot from behind Porus, looking past him at his infantry pressed by the Macedonian phalanx
panels.action: From behind Porus atop his elephant, we see his infantry struggling against rows of Macedonian spears pushing forward, the ground churned into sludge, his men’s faces strained and weary.

dialogue.text: Despite brave charges, Porus’s infantry bore the weight of the Macedonian phalanx. Discipline met discipline; slowly, under flanking horsemen, his line began to bend.

i18n.hi: बहादुर टक्करों के बावजूद, पोरस के पैदल सिपाहियों को मकदूनी फालैंक्स का पूरा बोझ झेलना पड़ा। अनुशासन से अनुशासन भिड़ा; धीरे–धीरे, किनारों से घुड़सवारों के वार में उसकी पंक्ति झुकने लगी।

panels.shot: tight close-up of Porus’s face, streaked with rain, eyes unbroken though surrounded by chaos
panels.action: Close on Porus’s face as rain streams down his helmet and beard; behind him the blurred shapes of retreating troops and restless elephants hint at mounting chaos, but his gaze remains steady.

dialogue.text: Greek accounts describe Porus wounded and his forces breaking, yet they note his refusal to abandon the field until he was nearly surrounded and his elephant exhausted.

i18n.hi: यूनानी लेख पोरस को घायल और सेना को टूटता दिखाते हैं, पर लिखते हैं कि वह लगभग चारों तरफ से घिरने और उसका हाथी थककर चूर होने तक मैदान नहीं छोड़ा।

panels.shot: low-angle medium showing Porus helping restrain his tired elephant as Macedonian troops close in cautiously
panels.action: Porus leans forward, calming and steadying his lathered elephant with a firm hand, while armored Macedonian soldiers approach in a wary semicircle, spears held ready but halted by a raised command gesture from their leader off-frame.

dialogue.text: At last, worn down, his elephant spent, Porus was taken. The sources are from his enemies, yet even they do not paint him as fleeing or begging for life.

i18n.hi: आखिर थके, हाथी ढह गए, पोरस पकड़ा गया। स्रोत दुश्मनों के हैं, फिर भी वे उसे न भागते दिखाते हैं, न जान की भीख माँगते।

panels.shot: wide interior of Alexander’s field pavilion, Porus standing tall in chains before an unseen seated figure
panels.action: Inside a richly furnished field pavilion, Porus stands, tall and straight despite shackles at his wrists, facing an unseen seated figure in shadow, Macedonian guards lining the tent’s edges.

dialogue.text: Brought before Alexander, the defeated king faced the conqueror not as a supplicant. Plutarch and Arrian preserve the brief exchange that followed.

i18n.hi: सिकंदर के सामने लाए जाने पर भी हारा हुआ राजा याचक नहीं बना। प्लूटार्क और एर्रियन उस छोटी–सी बातचीत को लिखते हैं।

panels.shot: medium close-up of Porus in profile, lit from one side, mouth set as he answers
panels.action: In profile, Porus lifts his chin slightly, eyes level, as he answers, the light catching the lines of strain on his face yet showing no bowing of his shoulders.

dialogue.speaker: king_porus_puru
dialogue.text: Treat me, Alexander, as a king.

i18n.hi: राजा जैसा, सिकंदर, राजा जैसा ही बर्ताव कर।

panels.shot: medium shot of Porus as guards begin to remove his chains, his posture unchanged
panels.action: Porus remains motionless as Macedonian guards move in to unfasten his chains, his gaze fixed ahead, body language unchanged, while figures in Greek armor watch with a mix of curiosity and respect.

dialogue.text: According to the Greek historians, Alexander was pleased by this answer. He returned Porus his kingdom, and even enlarged it, making a former enemy his ally in the Punjab.

i18n.hi: यूनानी इतिहासकारों के अनुसार, सिकंदर को यह जवाब भाया। उसने पोरस को उसका राज्य लौटा दिया, ऊपर से बढ़ा भी दिया—कल का दुश्मन आज पंजाब में उसका साथी हो गया।

panels.shot: wide shot of Porus back on his elephant, now amid a mixed host of local and Macedonian troops
panels.action: On a sunlit day after the battle, Porus once again rides his elephant, leading a column where Punjabi soldiers march alongside Macedonian units, the landscape open and green around them.

dialogue.text: From this point, the Hydaspes realm was no independent barrier but a client kingdom, its king ruling under Macedonian shadow, yet still a king upon his elephant.

i18n.hi: इसके बाद झेलम का यह राज्य स्वतंत्र दीवार नहीं रहा, एक आश्रित राज बना—राजा अब मकदूनी साए में था, पर हाथी की पीठ पर अब भी राजा ही।

panels.shot: evocative medium shot of Porus alone at dusk, overlooking the Hydaspes from a rise
panels.action: At dusk, Porus stands on a small rise overlooking the calm Hydaspes, his helmet off, hair loose in the breeze, as the river glows with the last light and distant camps dot both banks.

dialogue.text: Later tales in India would claim great victory; Greek texts speak of Alexander’s triumph. The truth in between remembers a battle hard fought, and a king who refused to beg even in defeat.

i18n.hi: बाद की भारतीय कहानियाँ बड़ी जीत का दावा करती हैं; यूनानी लिखतें सिकंदर की विजय गाती हैं। बीच का सच एक भयानक लड़ाई याद रखता है—और उस राजा को, जिसने हार में भी भीख नहीं माँगी।

panels.shot: final contemplative close-up on Porus’s face, aged slightly, eyes thoughtful, with the river blurred behind
panels.action: Close on an older Porus, faint lines at his eyes, looking thoughtfully toward the blurred ribbon of the Hydaspes behind him, his expression neither proud nor broken, but quietly resolved.

dialogue.text: Porus’s stand at Hydaspes did not halt Alexander, yet his bearing left a mark on enemy scribes. From their pages comes a lesson: true courage holds its dignity, even when the field is lost.

i18n.hi: झेलम पर पोरस की डट ने सिकंदर को न रोका, पर उसके तेवर ने दुश्मन लेखकों पर छाप छोड़ दी। उनकी पंक्तियों से सबक निकलता है: असली बहादुरी हार में भी अपनी इज़्ज़त थामे रखती है।

subtitle_i18n.en: At the rain-swollen Hydaspes, a Punjabi king met an empire without bowing his head.
subtitle_i18n.hi: बरसाती झेलम पर, एक पंजाबी राजा ने सिर झुकाए बिना एक साम्राज्य से टक्कर ली।
